key_insight: Replacing forward Fisher divergence with reverse Fisher divergence yields a much more favorable optimization landscape for Gaussian-mixture score matching, enabling global convergence of gradient descent under broad initialization conditions.
engineering_relevance: This suggests a more stable training objective for generative modeling and diffusion-style score estimation when mixture-based models or unnormalized targets are involved.
method: Analyze gradient descent on Gaussian mixture student models with fixed weights and identity covariances.
Use a Lyapunov-function argument to prove convergence for a single-Gaussian teacher from arbitrary initializations.
Extend to Gaussian-mixture teachers under global random initialization and a \widetildeΩ(1)-separation condition, showing component-wise alignment and TV convergence conditions.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports theoretical convergence guarantees: global convergence from arbitrary starts in the single-Gaussian case, and high-probability convergence with correct component matching under separated mixture targets.
watch_item: The strongest guarantees rely on model restrictions (fixed weights, identity covariances) and a target-mean separation assumption, so the results may not directly transfer to more general practical score networks.
paper_title: Global Convergence of Gradient Descent for Score Matching in Gaussian Mixtures via Reverse Fisher Divergence
